name: Homemade Salsa Picante
recipeIngredient: 1 bunch green onions, cut into 1-inch pieces
2 medium tomatoes, cored and cut into eighths
1 medium avocado, peeled and cut into 1-inch pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Place chilies or peppers on a baking sheet; broil 3 to 4 inches from heat, turning often with tongs, until blistered on all sides.
2. Immediately place chilies in a plastic bag, fastening securely and let steam for 10 to 15 minutes. Remove the peel of each chili.
3. Cut a slit in the side of each chili and rinse under cold water to remove seeds. Remove stems from chilies; quarter and set aside.
4. Remove husks from tomatillos. Place tomatillos in a small saucepan; add enough water to cover. Bring water to boil, then drain.
5. Cut tomatillos into wedges; set aside.
6. Combine all ingredients in the container of a blender or food processor. Process for 10 to 15 seconds or more if you desire a smoother consistency.
7. Chill before serving. Yield: about 4 ½ cups. May be omitted.
